notes: This is a curated sample of Ubuntu 24.04 packages that build against libzstd and also ship runtime functionality that relies on libzstd. It is intentionally not exhaustive.

packages.runtime_functionality: APT's core package-management library can fetch zstd-compressed repository metadata via Acquire::CompressionTypes::zst and can read .deb members such as control.tar.zst and data.tar.zst.

evidence.build_dependency: The apt source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: dpkg-deb can build and extract Debian packages whose control and data tar members use zstd, and dpkg can install packages that use .zst-compressed .deb members.

evidence.build_dependency: The dpkg source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: Rsync can negotiate zstd as a transfer compression algorithm and users can force it with --compress-choice=zstd/--zc=zstd, with zstd-specific compression levels via --zl.

evidence.build_dependency: The rsync source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: Systemd components use zstd-backed compression for journal data and coredumps; journald can compress large fields and systemd-coredump can store compressed coredumps.

evidence.build_dependency: The systemd source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: Libarchive can read and write zstd-compressed streams; bsdtar and bsdcpio can extract .zst input and create archives with --zstd.

evidence.build_dependency: The libarchive source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: The btrfs userspace tool uses zstd decompression for compressed send/receive flows, especially btrfs send --compressed-data and btrfs receive --force-decompress when handling zstd-compressed extents.

evidence.build_dependency: The btrfs-progs source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: mksquashfs and sqfstar can create zstd-compressed Squashfs images with -comp zstd, and unsquashfs and sqfscat can read or extract zstd-compressed Squashfs filesystems.

evidence.build_dependency: The squashfs-tools source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: qemu-img can create or convert qcow2 images with zstd-compressed clusters using compression_type=zstd, typically together with the compressed image path.

evidence.build_dependency: The qemu source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: Libcurl can automatically decode HTTP Content-Encoding: zstd responses; the curl CLI exposes this through --compressed, and libcurl exposes it through CURLOPT_ACCEPT_ENCODING.

evidence.build_dependency: The curl source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: LibTIFF supports TIFF images using COMPRESSION_ZSTD and exposes zstd compression controls such as TIFFTAG_ZSTD_LEVEL; tiffcp can write them with -c zstd[:opts].

evidence.build_dependency: The tiff source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.

packages.runtime_functionality: rpm, rpmbuild, and rpm2cpio can build, inspect, and unpack RPM payloads that use zstd compression, including zstdio payloads selected through rpmbuild payload macros.

evidence.build_dependency: The rpm source package Build-Depends on libzstd-dev.
evidence.runtime_dependency: rpm reaches libzstd1 through librpmio9t64 on Ubuntu Noble.

packages.runtime_functionality: zarchive can create and extract .za archives through libzarchive, exercising libzstd while round-tripping directory trees through zstd-compressed archive data.

evidence.build_dependency: The zarchive source package Build-Depends-Arch on libzstd-dev.
evidence.runtime_dependency: zarchive-tools reaches libzstd1 through libzarchive0.1 on Ubuntu Noble.
